reviews.text: I am writing to recommend John Leslie for the position of your attorney. As managing partner of my own firm, I have had the pleasure of working with John for over 20 years. I am a real estate developer of single family residential lots and an investor in healthcare facilities in the Dallas Fort Worth Texas area. I met John Leslie more than 20 years ago in Fort Worth through local civic and charitable organizations. John and I served as members of the Board of a local Non-Profit organization in Fort Worth Texas for several years. I consider him an honorable Christian man and I have observed him as not only an attorney but also as a loving husband, father, and grandfather. John has an exceptionally strong eye for detail that has been a benefit to our firm on more than one occasion. He also has a very calm demeanor that is a blessing for clients in and out of the courtroom. I believe John’s expertise and strong sense of organization would help him to gain the upper-hand in court and either settle or win a majority of his cases. John is an extremely talented attorney who cares a great deal about his clients. John has represented me in the creation and review of countless real estate documents as well as complex business issues.

reviews.text: Terrible service, I wouldn't recommend it to absolutely anyone. Extremely unprofessional and obviously inexperienced. They supposedly couldn't find the other car's insurance even though they had the license plate and other information. This information was given to us after they sent us to a clinic for medical tests and procedures. Now they've informed us that they can't continue with the case because they couldn't find the insurance of the car responsible for the accident, and we'll have to pay the clinic bills ourselves. In my opinion, they're scammers in cahoots with the clinic. I assume the first thing to do is find the car insurance company (which should cover all damages to both the vehicle and the injured parties) before sending the client for any medical procedures or other services. In conclusion, scammers, zero professionalism, zero ethics, zero experience. I don't know how they can call themselves lawyers (they couldn't logically answer any of my obvious questions since they obviously have no reason or explanation for such an atrocity). I hope my review helps others avoid falling into the hands of this so-called "law firm" and saves them time and money.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Lisaily,
We want to thank you for taking the time to give feedback about your recent experience with Kanner & Pintaluga. Please accept our sincere apology.
We would like the opportunity to investigate your feedback further. Please email us at customercare@kpattorney.com.
Thank you for your feedback.

A car accident is never a positive experience, but Chris and his team made it as painless as possible. They were with us every step of the way, responding quickly to our calls and questions with genuine care and professionalism. It was clear that their clients truly matter to them.

I hope I never need Chris’ services again, but if I or a family member ever do, Chris will be my first call.

reviews.text:
I had a wreck, and was referred to Adam. From the very beginning, he and his team made me feel completely comfortable putting my case in their hands.
My wreck was a result of an unlicensed, uninsured, teenager, in someone else's vehicle, pulling out in front of me, and the rest of flowing traffic. This incident caused a break in my sternum, and a loss of almost 2 months of work.
All I was really concerned about was making sure I wasn't going to be the one left with the medical bills.
Adam and his team started addressing this on Day One, and started addressing issues like vehicle replacement, initial (ER) medical expenses, and lost wages.
It took a well for everything to be wrapped up, which is fairly standard when dealing with insurance companies (especially when one of them insured a vehicle that a non-licensed driver was operating), however, in the end, I not only had all of my bills covered, but was also able to replace my totaled vehicle with a new one.
I have nothing but positive reviews for Adam and his team, and I would recommend them to anyone. They went way about what would normally be expected (in part from having to deal with the VA) and in the end, I could not have asked for a better outcome.
Thank you, Adam! Well done!

reviews.text:
I personally like Tracy, and she is knowledgeable in how trusts and estates work, but that is all I would recommend her for. They made me feel as if I were lying or being deceitful in some way while family attempted to railroad me.  They made me feel as if I should just rubber-stamp everything to make it easier for them, even though I could prove coercion and duress.  They didn't want to be bothered, and never actually had my best interests at the forefront of the interaction.

You are made to feel like a criminal for wanting to fight for what's right.   If your issue involves a dispute in regards to money, this office will victimize the victim.

It felt as if I was paying them to support the opposition, rather than fight for the one paying them.  It seems they are happy to make the same money pushing paper, rather than actually fighting for their clients.

Beyond happy I found different counsel that actually makes me feel supported and has my back!

Since they responded saying it was anonymous, I will happily leave my name here...Cheryl Breitman and I am a past client that was treated terribly.  If you care to discuss, you can look up my number in your records and call.  I will not go out of my way and waste another single second to appease your need to justify the actions of your staff.
reviews.ownerResponse:
This anonymous review does not reflect my understanding of the interactions of myself and the staff with any current or former client.   Please contact the office to review your concerns if you are a client.  One important role of an attorney is to prepare a client for cross-examination to ensure the case has the best chance of success.   Furthermore, the strengths and weaknesses of each case are important when advising whether a case is cost-effective to continue.  I am happy to discuss these issues with all current, past, and future clients so an informed decision when considering settlement can be made. 

EDIT. Thank you for identifying yourself in your revised post. I am unclear what must have recently occurred to justify this review. I will ask the staff if they knew of any of the frustration you expressed in the post. I will reach out after an internal investigation into this matter.

reviews.text: I live in San Diego and my aunt left me her house in Arlington.  I just blindly found Tracy on the internet and after talking with her I hired her. There were people squatting in the house and she handled everything for me.  She filed the eviction notices, served it personally to one of the squatters, and even went out with the Marshall on the day of eviction.   She kept me in the loop every step of the way and even sent me pictures of the inside of the house.   Tracy and her staff were professional, but also very compassionate as I went through this nightmare from 1,000 miles away.
reviews.ownerResponse: Squatters in a probate case create special headaches that deserve attention.   We approach each case as a team to do our best to get results.   Thank you for noticing the staff's efforts because their attitude makes all the difference.

reviews.text:
You know, in life it really helps if you have relationships with highly competent, high quality,  caring people. I have a mechanic and a contractor who have been trustworthy and dependable for years. I consider the Tracy Bush Law Office to be in that circle. Tracy and her staff have been so helpful, while at the same time being very caring as my family has fought its way through probates for relatives who died during this COVID scourge. In our case, we had to deal with California and Texas laws. In our consultations with Tracy, she laid out the possibilities and likelihoods. She also crafted a solid strategy. Like a soothsayer, some of her warnings about the negative possibilities came true, but her proposed solutions have saved us time and time again. I never knew how brutal this probate process could be, but it has been so wonderful having her there to hold advise us every step of the way. I can't help but feel that she truly cares about people, as she has met with us on very short notice when we needed quick answers. I highly recommend this firm and my family thanks the good Lord that we found her.
***UPDATE Nov 30, 2021***  It's a  mean old world out there when you are trying to handle legal matters. Ms. Bush helped us win a two-front war, both in Texas and another state. She delivered on her expected outcome and we can finally put this legal fiasco behind us. She was especially helpful with the banks, one of which was so bad that when we signed some legal documents, they waffled twice making us go back and change the signature. I don't know if we just got two different bank legal agents or what. Tracy helped us break through, and I feel we were getting the "runaround" from that bank. We can finally breathe a sigh of relief and move forward. Thanks to you and your staff, Tracy, all of whom seem to share your same high standards of excellence!
UPDATE: Years later, my family and I had to look back and write another note of thanks. We owe everything to Tracy Bush for helping us get through all sorts of hurdles with the legal system.

reviews.text: sent me on a wild goose chase to get something notarized and when I finally got to a notary nothing could be done because the form they sent me had last year's date on it.  now I don't know when I'll be able to get something like this signed.  just terrible.  wasted my time, wasted my money, wasted my energy.  would give negative stars if possible!  how do you, an attorney, whose job it is to dot every I and cross every T, send someone a form to sign in June with last year's date on it????
reviews.ownerResponse: I have no knowledge of any client being unable to have a document notarized that the Firm prepared and have no record of you retaining the Firm. When did you receive the document? Perhaps you received it in 2023 for one of my client's matters and are just now trying to notarize it, months later. Alternatively, notaries write in dates routinely without issue each January when a document was not signed before the change of year. reviews.text: Tracy handled all of our estate planning and is the absolute best attorney in the state of Texas for these services. Incredibly thorough and honest beyond reproach. I am so glad we met her when we did. Just an incredible person and a great team.

I sent a request to set up a meeting via email on their website. They contacted me and we spoke about my request and inquiry for my father and his estate. I spoke with Tonya, she was very short and did not even offer any options and was very matter a fact. She said we meet with dad only and Ms. Bush would not be able to see him for over a month. He can not even walk at this time and just got out of the hospital from having surgery on both of his legs. She was not helpful at all. She was done with me very quickly and off the phone. So sad.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I write this response to address your misunderstanding reflected in the Firm's ethical duties.
When another person, including a family member, initiates contact on behalf of a potential client and wishes to participate in the estate planning process, it creates questions of capacity and undue influence. Thank you for confirming in your review that Tanya followed this Firm's policies and ethical considerations required by the State. Although you desire a different outcome, Tanya cannot provide you advice over the phone because she is not an attorney. Further, Tanya's role was to ensure you understood that your father's desires would be the primary focus in any meeting and set the expectation that you would not be included in confidential attorney-client meetings if an appointment was scheduled. This is to prevent any potential conflict of interest and ensure all communications are kept in strict confidence. To your last concern about the Firm's calendar, it is important that the staff provide accurate information about the Firm's availability. This gives all potential new clients an opportunity to continue looking for an attorney who can provide faster assistance.

Outstanding SSDI Representation – Highly Recommended

After my SSDI application and reconsideration appeal were both denied, I turned to The Law Office of Daniel J. Gregory in Fort Worth, Texas. From the very beginning, Mr. Gregory and his team were nothing short of top-notch — professional, knowledgeable, and truly dedicated to my case.
They knew exactly what Social Security required, how to submit the right forms, and how to make sure all necessary medical records were requested and received on time. They encouraged me to stay involved as well, which I did, following up to ensure my records were sent in for review.
When my ALJ hearing approached, Mr. Gregory personally prepared me with the types of questions I might face, which helped me feel calm and ready. Thanks to their hard work and preparation, we won my appeal — and I was awarded unexpected amount in return of back pay.
Even though I live in Houston, the distance was never an issue. They handled my case from start to finish with the utmost professionalism, especially in helping veterans win disability cases.
To Mr. Gregory and his entire team — from me and my family, thank you. You gave us not just a legal victory, but peace of mind. I would highly recommend your office to anyone fighting for Social Security Disability Insurance benefits.
True professionals in every sense of the word.

By R. Gonzalez, USMC - Veteran Retired

reviews.text: Unfortunately I had the weirdest experience with this law firm….i did a consultation and was very clear and thorough, I was told that the firm decided to take my case ….im not sure what happened but the law firm must have contacted workman comp because all of a sudden workman comp went silent and said they couldn’t answer any questions from me because I have a lawyer that reached out to them….so I had no access to them anymore….so as the days and weeks went by I hadn’t heard anything from this law firm,I had to keep reaching out, then finally I asked the receptionist did they take my case or not because no one is interacting with me at all…I finally get Viviana but she had very little to say to me…she acted like she had no clue who I was and what I was talking about…I had to keep pushing the narrative of the things I wanted to talk about…she kept telling me to send her irrelevant random things that I thought she had already that’s why workmen comp stopped talking to me,…..so I couldn’t take it anymore I called Viviana for the last time and asked her did she take my case or not? Can you please tell me what’s going on? She had me explain again what I was suing for and who I was and she said…..” o , we don’t handle them kind of cases I’m sorry”….. I’m like errrrrrrrr? Whaaaat? After all this time ???? I’m devastated because so much time is wasted now I have to find another attorney….she didn’t offer a referral or anything…I was very disappointed….there were no communication with her, lack of compassion,short attention span and very dismissive….as you read this you can feel my disappointment…I’m glad to see all the other positive comments I wish I had one

I've done all the work. Several emails several calls trying to get someone who's really in charge. Then it's like they have a attitude. IT'S MY LIFE I'M FIGHTING FOR.  Then the attorney,  smh. Worse than they are.  He didn't want toto fight for me nor give the information to the doctor so it would strengthen my case. He didn't want to appeal my PLN-11.. he advised me to sell care on personal injury and it wouldn't be favorable to me. Did the judge trend to did with the carrier but I tell the viviana she says i don't know why he would tell you that.  The attorney didn't do anything he said.  I was suppose toto be set up with social security rehab program since jan.29th. Every time I ask there never any response. He isn't accessible.  Then my treating doctor office kept calling emailing and the office manager never got any response to the point she called me.  I then call back to this firm expressing the severity abs that I needed a higher up so they know from the client what's going on. But instead I was directed to that viviana person.  I told them I will be filing a complaint and I have emails calls where they are not responded too. Then too add insult to injury. I removed from my case but according to TDI they can't be removed right before  a BRC. The viviana person wanted to argue.. literally  got loud..It's funny how when someone no longer wants you.  People lose respect and I told the i want my file , no response as usual.  MLF YOU HAVE NO LEGAL RIGHT TO WITHHOLD MY MEDICAL RECORDS . They just kept getting paid and have done ABSOLUTELY NOTHING!!. Them thrifty had the audacity to act like I'm the problem. When someone keeps saying the same thing. You'd think maybe it's the other person.. I'm disgusted at these so called professionals.  The attorney was purposely trying to sabotage my case by bringing up things in the BRC that were irrelevant and not bringing up what he should. He tried to discourage me and tell me just go get help on the personal injury side.. why would I do that.  I want justice and my medical care because I didn't got someone with my truck. They hit me.  He supposed to work for the client.  Honestly this firm wanted to be rid of me because I WILL ALWAYS ADVOCATE FOR MYSELF PERIOD!!. people assume you know nothing. Oh that's the other thing. They only gave me information when I bring it to their attention. I expressed many times. I know your busy everybody wants the same thing and I repeatedly said, when it's my turn give me what I need you won't have all these constant mini difficulties.. then that viviana person thought she was going to get loud with me today and I hung up right in her face as  I did the receptionist. They played games. Keeping me from a higher up. Who does that. Maybe they good somewhere else in the firm but Jameson  don't use him for nothing!!. Stay away from this location. Go if you'll be passive and quiet but if your really trying to get help RUN..

**Review for Coby L Wooten**

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I had an exceptional experience working with Coby L Wooten after my recent car accident. From the very beginning, he showed a deep understanding of my situation and provided me with the guidance I desperately needed during a challenging time. In addition,  assisted me on calls with the insurance company representative.

- **Professionalism:** Coby L Wooten was incredibly professional and made me feel at ease. He was always prompt in responding to my questions and kept me informed throughout the entire process.

- **Expertise:** His expertise in personal injury law was evident. He took the time to explain every step of the legal process, ensuring that I fully understood my options and what to expect.

- **Compassion:** What stood out the most was his genuine concern for my well-being. Cody L Wooten not only focused on the legal aspects but also took the time to understand the personal impact the accident had on my life.

- **Results:** Thanks to his hard work and dedication, I received a settlement that exceeded my expectations. Coby L Wooten fought tirelessly for my rights and made sure I was compensated fairly for my injuries and losses.

- **Highly Recommend:** I highly recommend Coby L Wooten to anyone in need of a car accident lawyer. His combination of professionalism, expertise, and compassion makes him a top choice for legal representation.

Thank you, Coby L Wooten, for your support and for helping me navigate through this difficult time!

Sincerely,

JK Smith, Retired U.S. Navy Chief

reviews.text: Got hurt at work and it's a non subscriber case. Little girl who took my phone call was quick to tell me no before she even heard the whole story. I put in a request for a call back so I could talk to someone else and have yet to even get a call. No compassion..
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Cox/Blanco, I am sorry you feel that your questions were not answered adequately. I attempted to call you on August 13, 2025, personally, no one answered and I could not leave a message. Feel free to call me at my office number listed on the website. As a practical matter, work place injuries are catagorized as either workers comp cases or non-subscriber cases. My office will only look at non-subscriber cases. And if your case qualifies in that regard then it becomes a review of the facts of how it happened and then maybe more importantly the nature and severity of the injuries.
I try to return phone calls within 24 hours of a phone call being received, if possible. I did that. Please feel free to return my call if you would like to visit about your situation.
Thank you again for considering my law office.
Coby Wooten

Through countless sleepless nights and tear-stained days, Mary held my hand, not just as a lawyer but as a pillar of strength. Her unwavering support and genuine care have etched an eternal mark upon my spirit. With her by my side, I found the courage to fight, to believe that justice could prevail.

Mary Panzu, a name synonymous with hope, integrity, and resilience. She transformed my pain into purpose, my fear into fortitude. If you find yourself entangled in legal woes, look no further. I recommend her to everyone I come across that needs strength.

reviews.text: Finding a good lawyer is difficult, so having found a great lawyer, I feel obligated to share my experience. He was upfront about fees and potential outcomes, and he vigilantly monitored the status of my filing.  He demonstrated an exceptional work ethic and a dedication to follow-through.  It was clear that he was my advocate from start to successful finish.  If you are needing excellent legal counsel and a lawyer who willingly and consistently provides above and beyond service, I highly recommend Paul Previte.

5-star everything! I never had to go to court, didn't do much at all really.

1. Called, put down the initial money
2. They negotiated a settlement for a 90-day probation period for my ticket and I paid that total. Never had to go to court in my case, just an email saying to pay the settlement by a date provided.
3. Called the office once I received the settlement to pay. All in, $269.00 for an 83 in a 60mph ticket in the city of Arlington. ($50.00 initial fee, $213.00 for ticket, $6 for handling/payment fee)
4. They will text you the receipt.

The staff on the phone call was top-notch, friendly and assuring.

All I have to do now is keep from getting a ticket in Arlington for 90 days and my ticket stays off the record and disappears.

This lawfirm is AWESOME at what they do! Thank you to them and the city of Arlington for being fair, honest, and rapid at getting me resolution.

I will be using and referring people to them from now on. Outstanding job!

-JB

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. Mora Pérez,

As CEO of the firm, I personally reviewed your file after reading this comment. Frankly, I don't understand the purpose of this public attack, as the documented facts in your case directly contradict your claims. To protect the truth and my staff, I must respond point by point with the documented evidence:

1. "They only listen when they want money": False. Your last recorded payment was in July 2023. However, our most intensive legal work occurred months after you stopped paying. We filed your Asylum (I-589) in November 2023 and your Work Permit in January 2024. We continued working on your case and correcting administrative errors until April 2024, almost a year after your last payment.

2. "They don't do things right": The results say otherwise. We obtained approval for your Work Permit on February 19, 2024, less than 30 days after submitting it. Few law firms achieve such rapid results. While there were administrative errors (an incorrect date), my team (Celeste and Paola) filed the correction requests immediately. The legal objective was successfully met.

3. "They don't give clear answers": Our call logs from January 10, 2024, show that you spoke with our team and explicitly stated that you were "happy with paralegal Celeste's work." It's confusing that you would privately congratulate us on the legal work and then try to damage our reputation publicly.

I see from our notes that you have already hired a new attorney. I wish you the best of luck, but I offer some sincere advice: Don't judge your new representation by impossible standards of perfection. Publicly criticizing attorneys who are in good standing with the immigration bar does your case no favors, especially when your legal process is still pending and public information is available.

Despite your review, I am willing to work with your new attorney to transfer your file. I promise not to distort the facts as you have done to us; I am not a petty person. I sincerely hope you are able to remain in this country, and I believe a change of attitude will greatly help you in that endeavor.

Sincerely, Arvin Saenz, CEO

reviews.text:
Scammed twice. They can't do anything more than yourself can do. Save your money and take care of your case yourself. When I figured out that a man had stolen 25 thousand from me I sued the thief. But the thief was hiding inside his house avoiding the private servers that I paid to reach him. When I came in for a consultation the attorney promised me that they would find a different way to serve him. But it turned out that this attorney did the same thing that I had already done. He hired private servers that could never find the thief, so nothing have moved on. Because they could not do anything that I had not done it myself so I am exactly at the same juridic place that  I was one year ago. Except that I am short around 7 thousand more, because that is what I paid this law office for them to do nothing. They should have told me that they could not do anything
More than I had already done for myself if they were honest.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you Michely for taking the time to share your experience. We are truly sorry to hear how frustrating this process has been for you. We understand how upsetting it is to be the victim of theft, and even more upsetting when the legal process does not move as quickly as you hoped.

We would like to clarify a few important points that may help explain what has occurred. Under the Rules of Civil Procedure, a defendant must be properly served before a case can move forward. Proper service is not optional, it is a legal requirement. If service is not completed correctly, any judgment obtained can later be overturned, which could cause even greater delay and expense. For that reason, we must follow the rules carefully and precisely.

Unfortunately, when a defendant actively avoids service, such as refusing to answer the door or intentionally hiding, that is outside of our control. Evasive defendants often require multiple documented attempts by licensed process servers before alternative methods of service can even be requested from the court. Courts require proof of diligent efforts before authorizing substitute service, and that process can take time.
Hiring professional process servers was not “doing nothing”, it was a necessary legal step to build the record required by the court. Even when the attempts are unsuccessful, they are often critical in laying the groundwork for requesting alternative service methods.

We also want to respectfully address the suggestion that individuals should handle legal matters on their own. While people certainly have the right to represent themselves, civil procedure is highly technical. Missing a procedural step, serving improperly, or moving forward without court authorization can jeopardize a case entirely. Our role is to ensure the matter is handled correctly so that your rights are protected and any judgment obtained is enforceable.

We remain committed to working the case as required by the Rules of Civil Procedure and pursuing all legally available avenues. We would welcome the opportunity to speak with you directly to review where things stand and discuss the next procedural steps.

Again, we understand your frustration and appreciate your feedback. Our goal has always been to pursue your case properly, ethically, and in compliance with the law.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are very sorry you felt frustrated during the process. We understand that being involved in a legal case can be overwhelming and stressful.

After carefully reviewing your case, we would like to clarify a few important points. First, your case was resolved favorably. Although it may have seemed like more than two years, the timeline of your case was consistent with what we typically see in that county. Court schedules, opposing counsel delays, and procedural requirements often extend the duration of cases beyond what clients expect. Unfortunately, much of that time is beyond any attorney's control.
We also respectfully disagree with the assertion that you were "defending yourself." Preparing a case for court involves a considerable amount of behind-the-scenes work, including drafting documents, adhering to strict court rules, reviewing evidence, preparing legal arguments, meeting deadlines, and ensuring procedural compliance. The courts have very specific rules that must be followed, and failure to do so can seriously harm a case. Although much of this work isn't always visible, it's essential to achieving a successful outcome.

Regarding billing, it's not our policy to send threatening letters after a single late payment. When several consecutive payments are missed, our office sends a courtesy reminder in case the late payment was an oversight. We have a long history of working with clients experiencing financial hardship as long as communication is maintained. The suggestion that we are inflexible with payment arrangements doesn't reflect our usual practice and is misleading to the community. It's precisely this flexibility that has allowed countless clients to access experienced attorneys who would otherwise be beyond their financial reach.

We regret any misunderstanding regarding the conclusion of your case, but our records indicate that we informed you that we had concluded your case on November 12, 2025, and provided you with copies of the Agreed Final Divorce Order executed via email on November 12, 2025, and again on November 17, 2025, which is consistent with our objective of keeping clients informed.

We sincerely wish you all the best and thank you for giving us the opportunity to represent you.

Asked me questions and they don't do take workers comp cases
reviews.ownerResponse:
We're sorry us asking you questions created a bad experience for you. We ask questions because what may seem like a workers comp case isn't always that way, and so we need to hear more facts to determine what kind of case it is. If you are injured by equipment at work it could be a products liability claim. If you are injured by a coworker it could be a third party claim.

When you called we asked you to give us some additional details to help us figure out if it was actually a workers compensation claim. Once we confirmed it was a workers compensation claim we let you know that we could not handle the matter. Workers comp is a very nuanced field of practice and most attorneys that do it specialize in it, unfortunately we do not. Hopefully you were able to find a workers compensation attorney.

I do not believe that we have done business together. I do not recognize your name, and you do not point to any specific problem with me or my office. The slowdowns with COVID impacting case processing times have been hard on many families, and it has caused a great deal of stress and hardship on people's lives.

We have a strong reputation, and we do great work. I’m generally known for talking for over an hour at consultations to understand client’s cases, and I’m pretty confident that most of my clients will tell you that we treat clients like family. I’ve practiced immigration law for 15 years because I enjoy working with people, and we have a long-time reputation of charging reasonable fees and not being focused on the money.

If you do have questions, you are welcome to call our office anytime. Regardless, I wish you well. If it is family immigration delays that you are having issues with, I hope these resolve. It’s been a hard few years for many families.

I am writing to express my heartfelt gratitude for the exceptional legal representation and support you provided throughout my immigration process. Your expertise, dedication, and guidance made a significant difference in my life, and I am truly thankful for your invaluable assistance.
When I initially approached your law firm, I was overwhelmed by the complexities and uncertainties surrounding my immigration case. However, from our very first meeting, your professionalism and genuine concern for my situation immediately put me at ease. You took the time to listen attentively to my concerns and questions, providing me with comprehensive information and setting realistic expectations for the process.
Your knowledge and expertise in immigration law were evident throughout the entire process. You meticulously prepared and reviewed all the required documents, ensuring that every detail was accurate and complete. Your attention to detail and thoroughness gave me confidence that my case was in capable hands.
One aspect that truly stood out was your exceptional communication skills. You promptly kept me informed about any updates or developments in my case, patiently explaining the implications and offering insightful advice. Your availability to address my concerns and answer my questions, no matter how trivial they may have seemed, demonstrated your commitment to providing personalized and attentive service.
Moreover, I want to express my deepest appreciation for your unwavering support during the challenging moments of my immigration journey. Your reassurance and guidance gave me strength and confidence when I faced setbacks or uncertainties. Your empathy and understanding throughout the process made me feel supported and encouraged, making a significant impact on my overall experience.
Today, I am thrilled to say that I have successfully navigated the complex immigration process, and it would not have been possible without your exceptional legal representation and unwavering dedication. Your professionalism, expertise, and genuine care for your clients have made a lasting impression on me.
Once again, I would like to extend my sincerest gratitude for your exceptional service. Your commitment to excellence and your unwavering support have not only achieved the desired outcome but have also made a positive impact on my life. I will forever be grateful to you for helping me secure a brighter future.
I will enthusiastically recommend your services to anyone in need of immigration assistance.
Thank you once again for everything you have done for me.

reviews.text: Giving Matthew five stars is hardly enough. The word "thank you" falls short of expressing the gratitude my family and I feel. On May 13, 2022, after 14 years, I finally received approval for my U.S. residency. This process was very difficult for me because I had a bad experience with another lawyer. But from the moment I met Matthew, he gave me the confidence to explain my case. He listened to me and attended to me very kindly and patiently, explaining the steps to follow and helping me with the waiver process, which, with his help, was granted. Afterward, he prepared me for my departure and interview in Ciudad Juarez, Chihuahua. I'm back! My family and I are starting a new life thanks to him and his entire team. Matthew, being a great lawyer, has the perfect vision to choose each assistant who works for him—kind and competent people who attend to you and answer your questions patiently and in simple terms. I'm giving this testimonial so that everyone who, like me, is facing this process, doesn't get discouraged and can go to lawyer Matthew. I do this because I want to give back in some way for all the help he gave me and that I know he'll be willing to give to everyone who needs it. He sees you as a human being with a life to resolve, not just as a case to make money. I recommend him a thousand times over with all my heart. Thank you, lawyer Matthew. Thank you to your entire team that I met during these years. A great lawyer, a great team, thank you so much.

reviews.text:
From Australia on business - needed advise, made an appointment ~7 days in advance. Was called at 6PM the night before saying the lawyer wasn't going to be in and that I would be paying 100USD for a teleconference with him - no option to reschedule, no apology. Obviously promptly taking my business elsewhere.

Reply to response: Like I said, I was not given the option to reschedule, I was told the call would still cost $100 and I am in town for 3 months mate. Take the feedback and improve rather than make excuses.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Luke, I am sorry to hear that you feel that way. As you probably know, attorneys do not always have full control over their schedules. If courts reschedule our hearings last minute, we have to do whatever the judges ask of us. I had an out-of-state hearing that was rescheduled last minute. I’m aware that you were only in town for a few days, and that it was not possible for you to reschedule to meet with me the following week. That is why my paralegal offered you the option of having a 1-hour videoconference with me at your scheduled time or any day that week. We were just trying to answer your questions in the time frame that you had available during your trip. I know you were not interested in having a videoconference with me, and I know your travel schedule did not allow us time to reschedule for a time to meet in person.

I apologize for any inconvenience, but I would have loved the opportunity to discuss your case with you. I hope you enjoyed your time in the United States, I hope your legal matters were resolved well, and I hope you had good travels. If for any reasons you still need assistance, please call the office anytime. While we charge for in-office videoconference consultations, I’m always happy to talk and answer general questions over the phone; and, I do not charge for such calls.

I would highly recommend Benton Law Firm. I was in an injury car accident which was followed by a pile of confusing legal letters while my health insurance and car insurance companies tried to sort out liability. I knew I needed someone to help me navigate all the requests from the legal teams of these huge corporations. Jeff and Leon took on my case and were able to sort everything out for me. They were always available to answer my questions and kept me informed about my case status every step of the way.  They stuck it out with my case for two years and recovered the full amount available under my policies.  I would recommend them to anyone in a similar situation.

Helped me with my case years ago and got my social security started. Recently I’ve sent my son to him and I think he is just getting older and may have hearing problems because he disregarded a major situation that could have put my boy in harms way. However all is well. Roger Allen is one of the ones who cares. If you need help he’s one that will do extra and go the long way with you. He’s honest and committed.
He doesn't want me to explained what exactly my situation. He kept asked different questions even is not in the form about my daughter ssi.
Very helpful

Listened to my case, felt like he empathized with my situation, then, all the way up until my court appointment came up, the only thing he had done was help me get basic information. And when I actually GOT to court, he didn't say anything. Basically left me out to dry with practically ZERO advocation after I had waited almost a year and a half for the court appointment

If he sees you as a losing case, it seems he'll represent you long enough to see how much of a chance you have, then when the chances look bleak, he taps out from the case - at least that's what it felt like

He at one point tried to convince me to basically drop the case, and I had to ask him if he was actually trying to win, or if he was just going to keep trying to discourage me - which, in the end, he released me from his services not long after the court date, once I started doubting his efforts

My assumption is because I'm on the younger end of the spectrum, since he started to talk to me like I didn't understand the circumstances and suggested I just, "Go mow some lawns" for a livable wage. Which, aside from being insulting, is disconnected from how the world has actually operated for a while now, and shows how surface level his knowledge/empathy is when dealing with the very people he's serving

Long story short: Don't go here if you're young; he's old-fashioned and only fights for you if your case seems winnable. Don't know how he treats those who are elderly/older, but it's probably better than what I got
I was unable to walk for several years after an accident, and SSI kept denying me for disability over paperwork issues. Called him and in 3 months he had my disability approved.

I can empathesize with every 1 star review on here. Like them I'd give them 0 stars if i could. Notice how all of the 4 and 5 star reviews are very superficial and lack real detail.

I was rear ended in September 2022 and have only spoken to the office twice without me calling them, 1st when they came out to my home to discuss the case and 2nd when they needed information for my paperwork. The first time is the only time I've seen it talked to the actual lawyer. As someone else explained, they do send you a letter stating that you should not expect much communication because they are working on handling the case.

Well after almost 2 years since my accident, I have received no real updates unless I called and when I do, they basically tell me not to call anymore and they'll call me. Since this was my first experience working with a lawyer, I trusted them and have only called once every few months so you would think I was calling like every week or something.

After all of this time, my car was totalled and I ended up owing the car loan. Now they're telling me that they keep receiving offers so low for the medical part of my injury that it may not even cover the medical bills. They're saying that we all may need to go to court but I'm on the verge of looking for another lawyer because I don't even trust that they'll fight for my case when they know barely anything about me.

So far, worse experience and several of my friends have gotten into car accidents over the last couple of years and have received substantial settlements, which I wasn't even asking for. All I'm asking is that the expenses that I incurred be taken care of.  Why should I come out of my own pocket when I was the one rear ended???

I am thankful for the services provided by the Law Offices of David S. Kohm & Associates, retaining Tommy Carter in the practice helped me navigate a divorce successfully with smart advice and strategies that were in my own best interest not the interest of revenue generation speaking to his integrity. If you don't know where to start in the North Texas area I would start by calling Tommy!

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. Our records show that you submitted our online contact form, and our team attempted to reach you to offer a referral to a firm that handles mold-related claims. Unfortunately, we were unable to make contact.

If you would like the referral information or wish to discuss your situation further, you are welcome to contact our office directly. Our goal is always to ensure people receive guidance, even when their matter is not one we handle.

My experience began after a car accident in January 2022 in which an uninsured unlicensed motorist ran a yellow light and totaled my car. Several people recommended Brandy Austin..but my experience has been nothing but disappointing. Communication with this office doesn't exist. Several times my physical therapy was put on hold for weeks at a time while we waited for the attorney's approval...and it was almost always me who had to call the attorney's office multiple times just to get them to follow up with my physical therapist. I was constantly lied to about being called back...and after a week I would have to call back again.

It took an entire year after my physical therapy ended for an attorney to contact me about the settlement. He made it sound like they wouldn't take this to court and the offer the insurance provided was the best option. I was told that the rest of the process wouldn't take anytime at all...a few weeks later they received the settlement check from the other party. Now 7 months later...I have yet to receive anything. Each time I contact the office I'm told the attorney is out, on lunch, or "will call me back". Through their document portal I can see a Final Settlement Statement that has been the same since January...and somehow after the 33.33333% Attorney Fee (which they absolutely do not deserve), I'm being charged over $700 in additional fees. One such fee being the certified police report I had to furnish myself!

At this point I'm assuming the worst and filing a complaint with the State Bar of Texas. I just hope no one else has to experience this level of professionalism again.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Schreck,
I am sorry to hear your experience has been negative. I will investigate the matters you have complained about and reach out to for a resolution. Of note, the police report furnished by you is not in an acceptable format for court-related proceedings. The same goes for all medical bills and records, which must be under affidavit and in a specific format. All of the medical providers and the police department set their own prices. We have no control over those fees. These are required to obtain a sufficient settlement.

Additionally, as a part of our service to you, we negotiate your medical bills to a lower price to allow you to receive more in your pocket. This can take time as it requires multiple stages of approval from various sources. We can always just pay the full amount, but you would receive less, potentially.

As for communication, I show telephonic communication with you through our phone logs during July, June, May, April, March, February, January, December, November, October, September, August, and June. I can provide that information to you through Clio.

Again, I will look into your matter more closely and my office will be in touch to discuss the next steps so hopefully we can come to a resolution quickly.

Graham's Law Office because he is a genuine expert in his field, a Christian, a former US Marine, he earned his JD from Dedman School of Law at SMU, and I really like to support local small business owners. Here's why it's ultra important to do business with local experts /small businesses: Before I met Peter, I had a horrific experience from a nationwide ticket defense firm (initials OTR) and their clueless rookie Attorney where I paid in full in advance then was later dumped at the worst possible time as a client for logistical reasons since all his court cases were in another city. Trust me, you're 10 x better off with a veteran local Attorney who is a ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ expert in his field like Peter J. Graham. reviews.text:
I was involved in a car accident around October 2023. I contacted McCormick law firm to take care of my expenses. I had a hospital bill from that accident. When I got my settlement, I asked my lawyer Cole Fidler about that bill because the amount I was receiving will not cover my bill. The settlement says for the hospital bill "not paying per client". He said, do not worry. You do not have to pay for it. The party at fault will take care of that. I kept receiving letters from the hospital asking for that bill and I thought that it will be taken care of. A couple of months later I got a collector letter asking me to pay that bill. When I reached out to Cole Fidler, he told me that in deed it was my responsability to take care of that bill. When I confronted about his words that said I do not have to pay that bill, he responded that I did not have to pay that bill with the money I got from the settlement. I could use my own money to pay. He also suggested talking to collection agency and asking for a deal where I don't have to pay the whole amount.

In addition, my sister was involved in the same car accident and another lawyer from that firm took on her case. Same situation as mine but she did not pay her hospital bill because the party at fault took care of that.

I think I got a lawyer that dropped the ball in my case and my sister got the good lawyer. This firm is not serious.  They did not represent me and gave me false information in order to make me sign the settlement without fighting in my case.
reviews.ownerResponse: Your bill was not paid by us because you told us to send you a document that said "not paying per client"... your sisters bill was paid because of health ins. Send it to your health ins if applicable. - Atty Cagney McCormick

reviews.ownerResponse: We take all inquiries very seriously and understand that being terminated is a traumatic experience. Unfortunately, Texas is an "at will" state, meaning that not all terminations (including "bullying") are legally actionable. Sometimes that is hard to hear, even though true and accurate. Litigation is expensive and arduous and we don’t want anyone to be misled about a potential claim. We wish you the best.

I can’t even begin to tell anyone who may be reading this just how much I appreciate and how grateful I am for Susan Hutchinson and the law firm that she has built. She is tenacious and ever giving of her time and energy to fight for those who she represents.
I am a disabled man and I have been through the penal process of our country more than a few times. It was not always easy but just knowing that I was directed to Susan and her team by the God whom I am not ashamed to name. That being said Jesus Christ is the greatest advocate in the history of mankind and He never turns His back on anyone. And He will go out of His way to round me back up when I get a little off course.
Susan took my case and in doing so she gave me hope for the future knowing that there is people that still believe in those of us who may have made a few unhealthy choices in our lives. We are the 2% of the population who just can’t seem to wrap our minds around the fact that God’s put the authority in the world for a reason. And as I have come to know,when you are doing right that there are no reasons to fear the authority because they are for the good of the people. But that being said there’s always those who are bad apples in the mix. And that is the one that Susan is not afraid of taking on. There’s a fine line between church and state and I truly believe that Susan is definitely in tune with the spirit of our creator. She knows just how to go about taking on the big fish that thinks that they can swallow up the little guy. When one is held in captivity for the choice he made it is not a good thing to have to go through. And when we are treated like animals and put through inhumane situations then rest assured that Susan Hutchison is not afraid of the giants in the land. She will go toe to toe with them and she will win if they are wrong. Gods on her side for defending the ones who need defending and He never loses in a fight for someone who is being treated badly. He gives His grace and He sends to them the help they need.
Nobody else would take my case cuz my mom tried all the big wigs of the system,but Susan never batted an eye when she found out about what was done to me. And she made them pay.
God bless you Susan, Martha,Julie yall are a real blessing and Godsend. I thank you from the bottom of my heart and am eternally grateful for your support and service in the past few years. It all comes around don’t it.
The wheels of justice are always turning just sometimes it seems that they’re turning really slow. You are the best. Thank you.
Your friend Daniel
Ps by the way,today was payday and 6 years ago is when it all started. There’s a process and I say if you will trust the process I encourage you to trust Susan and her team. They know what they’re doing

She never mentioned her name either,  I see why..... Feb-07-2024. 1:55pm
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi, this is Susan Hutchison.  What we attempted to explain to you is that Texas is an “at will” state, meaning that an employer can hire, fire, or demote a person for any reason at all—good, bad or no reason except that the reason cannot be based on age, sex, race, religion, national origin or a disability.  Because of that, I ask our intake people to get specific information to determine if there is, indeed, a legal cause of action.  Most people don’t understand that they are an “at will” employee and we try and explain what that means and get their additional information so we can help them decide if it is something they want to pursue and if they have specific evidence to support a claim.  Lawsuits are time consuming and exhausting for anyone and we think it important that clients be informed up front.  I can see that you were not comfortable with that process but I do wish you luck in finding representation. Susan

As we no longer lived in the North Texas area where the injury occurred, he promptly returned any phone calls or emails.  My wife needed surgery for her injuries and at a time we didn’t have medical insurance that would cover the expenses then bill the at-fault party’s insurance.

Armin handle the insurance companies for us but also was able to make arrangements with a highly skilled surgeon to handle the delicate foot injury surgery and wait for payment for services rendered until the claim was settled with the insurance companies.

Not only did he obtain the full coverage amount from the at-fault party’s coverage, he went further with our insurance carrier for under-insured motorist coverage.  Excellent service all around, very satisfied with his results, would highly recommend Armin and I sincerely thank my friend for the excellent referral for Armin.

To make matters worse their legal assistants and staff did not do a good job of returning my calls in a timely manner. I had to make follow up calls and leave messages. I won't be using them again.

Don't expect to get a response if you are a client.  Don't expect to be informed about your case, even when you have asked and they haven't responded in months. If your attorney leaves the practice and they do send you an email stating your case will be assigned to another attorney within the firm, it won't be. When you send an email and ask which attorney your case has been assigned to, they won't respond. They just will not communicate with clients but they will send a bill each month. My bill was paid each month but I bet they'd respond if it wasn't. If this is the firm you want to retain, please do so. Your experience may be completely different but my experience, as a client was terrible.  The opposing party and I were in agreement with the action and the paperwork amounted to a few pages.  There is no reason for this action, that was agreed to in January, has not been finalized by the end of August.  And, there is no reason for a firm to fail to communicate with a client.

reviews.text: Absolute joke of a law firm. After continuing to call and even on video me getting fired for filing a complaint 2 separate managers admitted to it on camera. I emailed documents to support my case, which I have a very black and white open-shut case on my hands, and during the consultation the attorney said he’d get back to me in 10 days and he’d most likely take my case. Fast forward 2 months and I’m having to email and call them just for a follow up. The first time I called the receptionist said they had an emergency trial which delayed the process. I respected that. She said she’d follow up with me the next day regarding my case and once again I heard nothing. I waited 2-3 weeks and called back. This time a guy answered and said there was no one available to take my call at the time and he’d have to leave a message for me. I got a call back the next day and they said they still had not looked over my case after 2.5 months when I was quoted 10 days! I told her that I’d give them one more week to give me an answer because I could’ve already served my employer by now. A week goes by and she calls to say they won’t be representing me because of their case load, which they could’ve been more transparent about months prior, wasting my time when I could’ve been looking elsewhere. And it’s a shame too, because this was such an easy case for them and they could’ve made easy money.

I don’t recommend this attorney unless you have lots of time to waste.
Great company! Mrs Hermann is amazing! Great representation!
Herrmann Law is no good. Shut down Herrman Law today please. Adiós ✌🏼
They just pick and choose what case they want. Don’t actually care about the people

I reached out for guidance with a hiring situation and believe a paralegal answered the call. I have no knowledge as to how the process of seeking legal help works since I’ve never had to. I was quite nervous especially given the hiring situation I am having to deal with. It is not an everyday thing for regular people to be seeking legal help just because. I am aware that the paralegal is not the lawyer; therefore, it is not her job to do anything other than take my information down. However, she was not very welcoming. I felt like I had inconvenienced her day by calling. As I was explaining to her the reason for calling and possibly seeking legal help, she completely cuts me off. I felt so bad. I’m sorry if I was maybe talking too much, but I was trying my best to properly explain the situation. She says that she will leave a message and straight up hangs up…she didn’t care to ask if I had any further questions or concerns…which I did. Not even a, “have a good day” at the least ??? I didn’t even get a chance to properly thank her for at least answering the call. I can’t judge the character nor expertise of the lawyers because I didn’t get that far. However, as a first time impression, it was not a very good one.

reviews.text: We used Herrmann Law for a class action lawsuit. At first they were really good. Then they stopped returning  calls an emails. They sold out to the people we were suing. Do not use this  this firm unless you want to lose.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am very disappointed to learn we did not meet your expectations. As you know, you were part of a class action that involved more than 450 employees. Despite the class size, which included several hundred class members, and the multiple phone calls we received from you on a weekly basis, we made certain to return each of your phone calls in a timely fashion (within 1-2 business days).

As for your allegation that we “sold out to the people” you were suing—nothing could be further from the truth. Your case was hotly contested involving very complex and unsettled legal issues. Although your former employer fiercely contested the claims, we fought vigorously for you and your co-workers, and after months of negotiations we finally reached a preliminary agreement with your former employer to pay a generous settlement to more than 450 individuals. However, because your case was a class action, any settlement could not become “final” until it was rigorously reviewed by the federal judge assigned to your case.

In addition, as part of the settlement, we sent every class member, including you, a notice about the settlement and gave you the option to opt-out of the settlement and/or hire an attorney of your own choosing. After you received notice, you did not opt-out, and instead chose to accept the settlement that was offered to you.

Although I am disappointed to learn that we did not meet your expectations, we welcome your constructive criticism as we are constantly striving to be the best advocates for our clients. If you have any questions or would like to provide any other feedback, I would be happy to discuss with you. Feel free to send me an e-mail or call our office: 817-479-9229.

Best regards,

Drew Herrmann

If you’re looking for an honest and trustworthy law firm to help you get back on track, Walters Law Office is the one. From the first call, they take the time to explain every step of the process clearly and ensure you understand your options. Their team doesn’t just handle your case—they guide you through it, making sure you’re informed and confident in every decision. They genuinely care about their clients and always recommend the best course of action for your specific situation. I highly recommend Walters Law Office to anyone seeking legal support from a firm that truly values integrity, transparency, and client care.

I've read these other reviews & they're truly unbelievable to me.
My experience was awful. I hired Jack Walters after my original attorney moved out of state. My case was long & complicated. On our first meeting, I explained my expectations & urgency and Jack agreed. After our meeting, I felt confident he was the right choice. Plus, he'd been recommended to me by another attorney.
That was the last time I ever experienced confidence in Jack or saw any type of professional standards of communication, ethics or knowledge. And he was over my case for over a year.
First- almost everything he sent me & legal documents he sent to the defendant side had typos, misspelled words and even referenced old, former cases of his because he was too sloppy to proof read and edit those references out. Truly unbelievable and embarrassing.  I'm sure the opposing side thought we were a joke.
On top of this, his communication was non-existent. He didn't answer some of my most important questions, even with follow up.
Most of all, he never learned the details of my case, never fought for me and ultimately, Jack Walters ruined my case because of this. Each time I told him something huge in my case, his response was, that's good, be sure to send that to me. And my reply was I have sent it, three times and told you twice already too. His reply, I've got 80 cases I'm working right now, I can't remember every case. Okay... fair. But at least carefully review before each meeting, prep of document, mediation, etc. Good grief.
I chose to settle my case for a few reasons but the biggest was because I had zero confidence Jack would ever learn my case details or fight for me.
There's more but these were the biggest issues.
I let the attorney who recommended him know all of this as well.
Absolutely HORRIBLE experience. Do not recommend.
This guy is 5 stars really knows his stuff

Well, this is unfortunate. I will be Mr. Jack Walters first low rating. Even more unfortunate due the fact that he was recommend by another lawyer who was incredible.

Unlike others who have great reviews or Mr. Walters of his promptness and communication, I have had the complete opposite, and it was after a great virtual call.

A 15 minute Google Meets meeting where Mr. Walters expressed he wants his clients to see his face to connect with him, only to not have the 14-day follow-up he promised after my car accident report was released. I had even sent 2 follow-up emails to his front office with pictures and was told that Mr. Walters would reach out. This was all about 2 weeks ago.

My deduction at this point, is that Mr. Walters is not interested in my case, or I may not have a case, and his new method of turning down a case is full on ghosting.

It is a shame too.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Arcelia, we apologize for the lack of communication. Given that we have one attorney in our office, sometimes communication is delayed due to the attorney having to attend court hearings, depositions, and mediations. In your matter at hand Mr. Walters was out of the office in depositions and mediations for the majority of the time you complained that you did not receive anything back.

We have a duty to our current clients to respond to them in a timely fashion before we can attend to potential clients, such as yourself. Again we apologize for the miscommunication and will strive to improve our communication process in the future.

I will do my best to just state the facts. I leave it to you to make your own fair judgment on the time & cost VS product/services.
SITUATION:
In Oct 2024, I infomred my wife I wanted a divorce. We spent Oct-Dec peacefully dividing 95% of our assets. But became stalled on an agreeably cash value for her TRS (Teacher Retirement, pension). We agreed to each retain a lawyer and let then negotiate a fair cash value of the TRS Pension. Then they could finalize ALL our already agreed on divided items in a final divorce decree. We thought this to be a simple & quick solution...

I reached out to several of my Buisness and Realestate attorneys and they all mentioned one name as the best in DFW area: "David Kulesz". I called to set up a consultation with Deavid and was told he had retired and was no longer taking cases. But a Brooke Wilson was avialable. Being that the assets were 95% divided and uncontested, I agreed to meet.

On Feb 5, 2025 I attend a consultation with Brooke Wilson. I explained the simplicity of my situation. Brook explained the process should take an estimated 2-3 months and would cost about $5000. She appeared very confident and eager. so I agreed to retain her office, signed all the needed forms and advanced her office $5k to complete the task.

On Feb 6 I emailed Brooke requesting her office to formally file the Divorce in Johnson county.
On Feb 26 I emailed Brooke my offer to settle the disputed value of my wives TRS, with instructions for her to present this offer when she speaks with the opposing council, to set up a mediation date and time. Thus, 1 call accomplishing two objectives.
On Feb 28 I recieved an email directing me that Brooke had spoke with opposing councel and both agreed mediation was needed. No reply/mention of my offer.
On March 1 I recieve a request to complete a standard divorce inventory form.
On March 5 I emailed Brooke the inventory form but with only the item being contested.
On April 29 I email Brooke with my expectations of presenting offer(s) in hopes of settling without/before a mediation date. She replied requesting to prep the offer in the form of a divorce decree and send it to opposing councel VS a formal offer statment. I replied with agreeing to that request.
On May 13 I email asking for an update? Received a reply that the offer was being drated.
June ? Received a draft copy of thr offer.......
June 18 I emailed Brooke a atorney/client termination letter.
July 16 I revceived a refund chk estm for $100
On June 20 I called the courts clerk request information on my case and was informed that my divorce papers were filed but lacked a responce/reply from my wife. Thus she had not been legally served. During questioning my wife about this lack of responce, I was told opposing council claimed she never received any offers to settle.
In short 4 months and some $5000, I terminated the partnership and walked away with only divorce papers being filed. Maybe I am wrong to be dissatisfied and this is common practice/timeline in the Divorce law......
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. While we can’t address the specific details of any case publicly because of client confidentiality rules, we do want to clarify a few general points that may be helpful to you or others who read this review.
In Texas, there is a mandatory 60 day waiting period after a divorce petition is filed. That means our attorneys will not guarantee a quick turnaround—especially when even one issue remains unresolved. Even when couples agree on most items, the remaining disputed portion is often the most difficult part of the case and can take longer than clients expect.
We also want to note that some of the statements in the review don’t match the information in our records, including the financial figures and timeline facts. What we can say is that we work very hard to set realistic expectations with every client and to keep communication clear throughout the process. We never promise specific outcomes, but we do make every effort to guide clients through each step and explain what to expect.
If you’d like to discuss your experience further, we would be more than willing to review everything with you privately. Our door is always open, our phones are always on, and we’re happy to walk through what was done, when it was done, and why.
Thank you again for taking the time to comment.

Here’s the updated version of your review with that new detail added in:

---

**Review of Attorney Carlos Lopez and Firm**

Hiring Queenan Law Firm and being represented by Mr. Carlos Lopez was one of the most disappointing legal experiences I’ve ever had. Not only was I charged the 33.33% contingency fee stated in the contract, but he also added an additional **$3,457.34 in “other fees”**—after previously telling me it would only be $2,300 before I agreed to settle; mind you that it is not even us going to trial.

On top of that, Mr. Lopez got me into settling by telling me that if I went to trial, the other party would uncover my past workers’ compensation during discovery and that I would likely receive less money. I now see this as a tactic to push me into a settlement I was not comfortable with.

He also gave me **inconsistent and misleading information** about the at-fault party’s insurance policy. At first, Mr. Lopez told me the other party carried a **$100,000 policy limit**, but later he changed his story, saying he wasn’t sure and that it might only be **$50,000**. This kind of back-and-forth left me feeling like he was not being honest or simply not competent.

Throughout the process, I felt misled, mistreated, and at times like I was dealing with an **inexperienced intern rather than a licensed attorney.** Lies, miscommunication, and broken promises defined my entire experience. I even reached a point where I just wanted him off my case completely.

Because of this conduct, I will be filing a **formal complaint with the Texas State Bar** against Mr. Lopez. What he did was not right, and no client should ever have to go through what I experienced.

**DO NOT trust this firm with your case, and especially do not trust Mr. Carlos Lopez.** He did me wrong, and I would never recommend his services to anyone.

You could not find more capable and caring attorneys! Kevin Queenan, Carlos Lopez, and the rest of the team leave no stone unturned in their meticulous preparation, investigating every possible angle to secure outcomes that consistently exceed expectations. I would highly recommend them.
